nativeRange.habitat: Lakes, streams, and shallow wetlands across the southeastern United States, extending into northeastern Mexico

decorPreferences: background or midground placement in groups

summary: Water Hedge (Didiplis diandra) is a fine-leaved North American stem plant native to the southeastern United States and northeastern Mexico. Its needle-like foliage develops bright red tips under strong light with CO2 supplementation, making it a striking choice for background or midground planting in nature-style aquascapes.
careNotes: Water Hedge belongs to the family Lythraceae (the loosestrife family) and is the only species in its genus — an uncommon distinction that makes it one of the more botanically singular North American stem plants found in the hobby. It produces thin, needle-like leaves on slender stems and is best planted in groups for a bushy effect, with regular trimming to encourage lateral spread. It is a slow grower compared with many stem plants, which helps maintain layout stability but means it does not aggressively compete with algae; keeping nutrient levels balanced and pruning any algae-affected lower stems promptly will help it stay healthy.
breedingNotes: Propagated vegetatively: cut anywhere along a healthy stem and replant the cutting in the substrate. Frequent topping also encourages lateral side shoots from the mother plant, producing the bushy hedge-like growth the common name refers to. Emersed flowering is possible but seed propagation is uncommon in the hobby.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Used for lighting (moderate-high), CO2 (not necessary but enhances red tips), growth rate (slow), pH (6.0-7.5), height (4-12" ≈ 10-30 cm), care level (easy, averaged up to intermediate), propagation (cut anywhere along stem), and native range (USA lakes and streams). Also confirms family Lythraceae and the 'Peplis Diandra' synonym.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Didiplis
additional.notes: Confirms monotypic genus in family Lythraceae, order Myrtales. Native range 'Southeastern USA to Northeastern Mexico,' extending the Buce Plant 'USA' range to include Mexico. Binomial authority: (Nutt. ex DC.) Alph.Wood.
